sections.literary-appearances: Romance of the Three Kingdoms: 106–107, 109–110, 114Sanguozhi: Wei 3
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cao Fang was styled Lanqing.

Cao Fang had the longest reign of the Wei emperors, but he had little authority.

In 254, he was accused of extremely inappropriate conduct by Sima Shi and other court officials. Empress Dowager Guo was pressured to have him deposed. After his deposition, Cao Fang returned to Qi and regained his former feudal title of the Prince of Qi.

When Sima Yan replaced Wei with the Jin dynasty in 265, Cao Fang was demoted to Duke of Shaoling County. He died in 274 at the age of 42.

full_text:
Cao Fang (Lanqing) 曹芳 (蘭卿)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Fang (曹芳), styledLanqing (蘭卿). Succeeded Cao Rui. He was deposed after being accused of abhorrent acts by the Simas. Cao Fang was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom. Cao Fang lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

Cao Fang was styled Lanqing.
Cao Fang had the longest reign of the Wei emperors, but he had little authority.
In 254, he was accused of extremely inappropriate conduct by Sima Shi and other court officials. Empress Dowager Guo was pressured to have him deposed. After his deposition, Cao Fang returned to Qi and regained his former feudal title of the Prince of Qi.
When Sima Yan replaced Wei with the Jin dynasty in 265, Cao Fang was demoted to Duke of Shaoling County. He died in 274 at the age of 42.
